idea-plugin.name: SecretLoader
idea-plugin.description:
SecretLoader pulls application secrets from a secrets manager and supplies them to your run and debug sessions as environment variables, so credentials don't have to live in appsettings.json, .env files, or run-configuration dialogs. Your code reads them like any other environment variable, and nothing about how you run the project changes.

It works with any command-line tool that can print secrets as JSON. Infisical is configured by default, and presets are included for HashiCorp Vault, Doppler, and AWS Secrets Manager. A command template adapts it to an in-house tool:

  • {cli} export --format=json --env={env} --projectId={project}

It has been tested with Infisical; the other vaults run through the same generic engine.

Switching environments

Move between dev, staging, and prod without touching code. The active environment is resolved by precedence, highest first:

  • a SECRETLOADER_ENV variable set on the run configuration
  • a per-project setting
  • a git-branch to environment mapping in .infisical.json
  • the repository default, then the global default

Details

  • If secrets can't be loaded, the launch is stopped rather than started against blank configuration. An environment that legitimately has no secrets still launches.
  • A per-project, in-memory cache keyed by project, path, and environment, with a configurable TTL.
  • A key blacklist to exclude specific keys from injection.
  • Secret values are kept out of console output.
  • The CLI is run directly rather than through a shell, and the environment and project id interpolated into the command are validated first.
  • An optional pre-commit check that blocks commits containing known secret values.

In the editor

  • No changes to application code or run configurations.
  • A status-bar widget showing the active project and environment.
  • One-click detection of the project id from .infisical.json.
  • A dry-run panel that previews which keys will be injected.

Works across IntelliJ-based IDEs, including IntelliJ IDEA, JetBrains Rider (.NET), PyCharm, WebStorm, and GoLand.
